title: Wazuh
description: Open-source security platform delivering unified XDR and SIEM protection for endpoints and cloud workloads, with a web dashboard for alerts and agents.
installation: This template deploys ONLY the Wazuh Dashboard (web UI, wazuh/wazuh-dashboard), which listens on port 5601 and is a thin front-end. Wazuh is a multi-container stack and will not function until you also deploy its companion services: 1) wazuh/wazuh-indexer:4.12.0 - the OpenSearch-based data store that holds all alerts and events (Wazuh's real datastore; it is NOT one of the supported operator add-ons, so no add-on is provisioned here - run it as a separate StatefulSet with its own volume and TLS certs); 2) wazuh/wazuh-manager:4.12.0 - the analysis/server engine that ingests agent data (exposes 1514/1515/55000) and provides the API the dashboard queries. The dashboard connects to the indexer via OPENSEARCH_HOSTS and to the manager API via WAZUH_API_URL; update those hostnames/ports to match the Services you create for the indexer (:9200) and manager (:55000). Generate the indexer/manager TLS certificates with the official wazuh-certs-tool before first boot. Rotate every credential in this template (INDEXER_PASSWORD, DASHBOARD_PASSWORD, WAZUH_API_PASSWORD and the manager/indexer admin passwords) away from the placeholder values. Log in to the dashboard at {{KUBERO_APP_URL}} with DASHBOARD_USERNAME / DASHBOARD_PASSWORD after the indexer and manager are healthy. Wazuh agents are installed separately on the endpoints you want to monitor and point at the manager.
